title: Rotational Dynamics
summary: How ships rotate and the tactical implications of rotational motion.
details: Rotation uses attitude thrusters, not main engines. Ships can rotate while maintaining trajectory - facing one direction while traveling another. Rotation rate limited by thruster power and ship moment of inertia. Elongated ships rotate slower than compact ones. Rotation continues until counter-thrust stops it. Plan rotations ahead - they take time to execute and stop.
annotations: Decoupled rotation and translation is key tactical capability.
Rotation speed often limits combat effectiveness.
